def: A soft white colour with a hint of yellow.
The curved band of colours seen when the sun shines through rain.
To come together with someone at an arranged place or time.
An instrument that measures temperature.
Open shoes with straps, worn in warm weather.
A small furry animal with long ears that lives in burrows.
A flying insect that collects nectar and makes honey.
The meal eaten in the middle of the day.
A soft yellow food made from cream and spread on bread.
A sweet white substance used to flavour food and drinks.
A round, starchy vegetable that grows underground and is eaten cooked.
A small, sweet red fruit with tiny seeds on its surface.
Newly made, picked, or produced; not old or stale.
Tasting like sugar; also charming or cute.
One of the hard white parts in the mouth used for biting and chewing.
The short, thick finger set apart from the other four.
Feeling unhappy or full of sorrow.
An unexpected event that causes damage or injury.
A natural substance in food that the body needs to stay healthy.
A machine that washes clothes.
A thick, warm cover used on a bed.
A clock that rings to wake you at a set time.
A marked place where buses stop to pick up passengers.
A place where books are kept for reading or borrowing.
A person who works in government or holds a public office.
Open to or shared by all people, not private.
Good at making new things or having original ideas.
The parts of the body that tighten and relax to make it move.
Busy and full of energy; taking part in activities.
A female friend, or a woman one is in a relationship with.
A woman you work with.
To begin to feel deep romantic love for someone.
An instruction that says what you may or may not do.
One of a pair of long narrow boards worn to glide over snow.
A place where people learn to ski.
A stiff boot that fastens the foot to a ski.
A person who rides down snowy slopes on a snowboard.
A warm, waterproof jacket worn for skiing.
Coverings for the hands, with a part for each finger.
The sport of riding down snow on a single board.
The state of the air outside, such as sun, rain, or wind.
Water that falls from the clouds in drops.
The space above the earth where clouds, sun, and stars appear.
A tall plant with a wooden trunk, branches, and leaves.
To wish for something and believe it might happen.
Needed; something you must have or do.
Already taken or in use; not free.
Without other people; on your own.
No more than a stated amount; as a maximum.
Not the same as the one already mentioned.
In a way that is not the same.
Made known to people in advance.
A group of things that share the same features; a kind.
Prepared and willing to do something.
By that means; as a result of it.
Inside that thing or place.
The main point of attention or interest.
The outer outline or figure of something.
Led or shown the way by someone.
Great physical force, often used to hurt or damage.
A number of people or things gathered or classed together.
Happening often.
A perfectly round flat shape; also a group or district.
Only; nothing more than.
For a long time already; a long time ago.
In most cases; most of the time.
For each one.
A formal talk given to an audience.
A line of people or things side by side.
Something that a person does; an action.
The name of a book, film, song, or other work.
Not easy to see or understand.
Made available to the public, as a book or article.
The particular way in which something is done.
A smaller amount; not as much.
A person who lives in or comes from Vienna.
Not including; apart from.
Not ordinary; different in an important or notable way.
Sure and definite; also used to mean 'surely'.
On this particular occasion.
The planet we live on; also the ground or soil.
A statement that makes something clear or easy to understand.
The season between winter and summer, when plants grow.
Firm and solid, not soft; also difficult.
A piece of land completely surrounded by water.
A place where people receive medical treatment.
The customs, arts, and way of life of a group of people.
A long thin mark; also a route of a bus or train.
A person who plays or writes music.
The place where someone or something is.
A particular way of doing a skilled task.
A trip that visits several places.
Two times.
Working by itself, without a person's direct action.
To find or notice something for the first time.
Used before the first of two choices.
In or to various places surrounding a point.
Involving two or more countries.
A shape or mark made of one line crossing another.
A team that represents a whole country in a sport.
Made or meant for one particular purpose.
Something unpleasant done to someone for breaking a rule.
A woman who sings, especially as a job.
The use of scientific knowledge to build tools and machines.
At or toward the front.
The best team or person in a sport worldwide.
A wonderful event that seems impossible to explain.
The act of making something different.
A warning signal of danger, often a loud sound.
In place of something else.
To greet someone in a friendly way when they arrive.
About; approximately.
Three times.
Within something; on the inside.
The amount of space between two places.
The event of a baby being born.
A part of a country or town; the surrounding area.
A small enclosed room, as on a ship or for changing clothes.
A round, solid object shaped like a sphere.
A female ruler of a country, or a king's wife.
The land along the edge of the sea.
A large road vehicle for carrying heavy goods.
A device with moving parts that does a particular job.
The popular style of clothes or behaviour at a given time.
During the morning; every morning.
A person who rides a motorcycle.
During the night.
Usually; in the normal way.
Belonging to one person or group, not open to the public.
